message:
perf(extension_dispatcher): fast-path bypass for io_uring and allocation-free shadow sampling

Optimize the hostcall dispatch hot path by short-circuiting io_uring lane
decision and telemetry when io_uring is not active. Previously, every
dispatch call computed queue telemetry, io hint classification, capability
class, lane decision, and emitted telemetry even when io_uring was
completely disabled. Now, when io_uring_lane_active() is false, the
dispatcher goes directly to dispatch_hostcall_fast() and skips all lane
machinery. Opcode entropy computation is also deferred to after dispatch
(it was previously computed before but only used for post-dispatch regime
detection).

Replace SHA-256 based shadow sampling bucket computation with FNV-1a64
hashing plus a final avalanche mix (splitmix64 finalizer). The SHA-256
path allocated a Sha256 hasher and produced a 32-byte digest per sampling
check; FNV-1a64 operates entirely on the stack with no allocations.
The avalanche pass (xor-shift-multiply) ensures good low-bit dispersion
for the modulus operation used in sampling probability.

Factor out io_uring_lane_active() as a const fn helper from
advanced_dispatch_enabled(), making the latter also const.

Add with_folded_ascii_alnum_token() locally for the dispatcher module and
convert shadow_safe_session_op(), shadow_safe_events_op(), and
shadow_safe_tool() from normalized_shadow_op() + matches! on String
to allocation-free byte-slice matching.

Add test infrastructure and 4 new tests:
- build_dispatcher_with_policy_and_oracle() helper for constructing
  dispatchers with specific policy/oracle configs
- dispatch_denied_capability_still_denied_when_advanced_path_disabled:
  verifies the fast path still enforces policy denials
- dispatch_allowed_capability_still_resolves_when_advanced_path_disabled:
  verifies allowed calls complete on the fast path
- advanced_dispatch_enabled_when_dual_exec_sampling_non_zero,
  _when_io_uring_is_enabled, _when_io_uring_force_compat_is_set:
  verify the detection logic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
